panel.basis: OOS RMSE 1.232 (free-anion) -> 0.516 (lipid), matching in-sample Tang-refit 0.519, with NO parameter touched for Tang (constants fit on Yamazaki). ~5x error reduction.

The dominant baseline miss is fixed at the mechanism level: PFOS (high-K_PL sulfonate) stalk 0.013->0.620 vs Tang 0.571 -- the ~40-200x under collapses, exactly as the K_PL-gated lipid term predicts; corroborated by Chen 2025 membrane K_MW.

Residual is honest and non-fatal: GenX (ether) stays over-predicted (provisional ether f_xy offset, a separate condition-dependent issue, not lipid loading) and PFOS endosperm still ~5x under -- but the overall and per-organ pattern transfers.

chief.basis: Out-of-sample generalization SUCCEEDS: the lipid-facilitated loading mechanism (K_PL-gated; constants fit on Yamazaki excl. PFDoDA, NOT on Tang) drops the independent-dataset Tang OOS log10 RMSE from 1.232 to 0.516 -- matching the in-sample Tang-refit (0.519) without fitting anything to Tang. It fixes the dominant free-anion failure (PFOS ~40-200x under -> spot-on stalk), consistent with the membrane-partition mechanism Chen 2025 independently confirms. This is the project's first strong cross-dataset out-of-sample predictive success and resolves the hyp-oos-tang REFUTED baseline at the mechanism level. Residual GenX ether over-prediction (provisional offset, separate from lipid loading) and a PFOS-endosperm under-prediction remain, so 'supports' (mechanism generalizes), not a perfect fit. Hypothesis supported.
